title: map_client_error vs map_put_error — contributor could use wrong mapper
description:
Scenario: a contributor adds a new Vault operation (e.g., delete, list) and uses map_client_error because it's what get uses, without reading the semantic distinction.

map_client_error maps 404 → NotFound (correct for get: path doesn't exist). map_put_error maps 404 → Backend/mount-not-found (correct for write ops: path not existing means mount is wrong).

A delete of a non-existent path should arguably return NotFound, not Backend, so map_client_error would be correct there — but a future list-secrets call where 404 means the mount doesn't exist should use map_put_error semantics.

The docstring on map_put_error explains the distinction well. Maintenance consequence: misclassified errors surface as confusing error types to callers (Backend when they expect NotFound or vice versa), generating issues.

Mitigation: the doc comment is good; consider renaming map_client_error to map_get_error to make the symmetry explicit and reduce the probability of accidental misuse.
